mnemonics: WMA = White noise Moving Average: Think of a Weighted Moving Average filter applied to a White Noise source.
keyPoints: A GLP is a linear combination of past and present white noise terms.
It is a foundational model that includes AR, MA, and ARMA processes as special cases.
The process is defined by its coefficients (ψ-weights), where the current value X_t = μ + Σ(ψ_j * ε_{t-j}) for j from 0 to ∞.
commonMistakes: Forgetting the requirement for coefficients to be absolutely summable (Σ|ψ_j| < ∞) for stationarity.
Confusing the GLP form (based on white noise) with a simple linear regression on past values of the series itself.
Assuming all GLPs are invertible; invertibility is a separate property from stationarity and depends on the model form.
